mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates expanding economic output, which historically correlates with rising corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth vs 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year window, or 20-day returns following GDP growth are not statistically different from other periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0%, it signals a tightening monetary policy stance that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods below threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals an overheating economy that prompts central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where both UNRATE is below 5% and RSAFS is growing >5% YoY, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During economic recovery phases characterised by rising INDPRO from a trough, further increases in industrial production signal robust supply-side growth, which is positively associated with S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: During periods identified as recovery (INDPRO rising from trough), the signal fails to produce positive 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.5%, it signals that the economy is overheating, prompting expectations of monetary tightening, which depresses equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%, 60-day returns are not significantly negative at 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market strength, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Payrolls growth has zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over rolling 3-year windows (Rank IC <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals a broad economic contraction, which strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both series decline simultaneously, 60-day returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns less than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime characterized by low unemployment (UNRATE <= 4.5%) and rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS trending up over 3 months), any additional increase in the fed funds rate amplifies negative market reaction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a two-week horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE <= 4.5% and FEDFUNDS is rising, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative compared to other regimes (t-test p > 0.05).
